is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum malaria epidemic in Kidal, Mali, confirmed by microscopy and PCR.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfcrt K76T (position 76), dhfr S108N/N51I/C59R (108,51,59), and dhps A437G/K540E (437,540) — all curated positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: pfcrt 76T in 45/57 (79%), K76 11/57 (19%); among 22 samples dhfr S108N 45%, C59R 35%, N51I 30%; quintuple 0%.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from Kidal district, northern Mali — a sub-national administrative unit; survey in 5 nomadic camps.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Cross-sectional survey conducted 11–18 October 1999, single time point (<=3y).

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates collected from patients in Kidal district, Mali (Africa) via finger-prick filter paper.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary cross-sectional field survey with original PCR data.

duplicate_risk.evidence: New field collection during a specific 1999 Kidal epidemic; no indication of consortium/network overlap.

supplement.evidence: Frequencies given in main text and Figure 1; no supplement required for extraction.

assessment.summary: Cross-sectional survey during a 1999 P. falciparum epidemic in Kidal district, northern Mali. Reports pfcrt K76T (79% in 45/57), dhfr triple mutations (S108N 45%, C59R 35%, N51I 30%; among 22 samples), and dhps A437G in one patient with no quintuple mutant detected. Sub-national location (Kidal district) and single collection month (October 1999) both satisfy resolution criteria.
